description_long: This object is a retro-style radio receiver with a rectangular, teal-colored body and slightly rounded edges. The front face features a large circular speaker grille on the left, a horizontal frequency display window with FM and AM bands, and three control knobs or buttons below the display. There is also a small blue indicator light and a toggle switch on the lower right. The sides and back are plain, with no visible ports or openings, and the radio stands on two small feet. The overall design is minimalist, evoking mid-20th-century aesthetics, and the materials appear to be primarily plastic with some metal components for the controls and internal electronics.
description: A teal, rectangular retro radio receiver with a front speaker grille, frequency display, and control knobs. The design is minimalist and vintage, with plastic and metal elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, frequency display, three control knobs, a blue indicator light, and a toggle switch.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the plain, solid teal side panel with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a smooth, unadorned teal surface with no controls or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain teal side, mirroring the left side with no additional features.
